articles.title: Tesla Will Unveil Model Y L 6-Seater On April 22 In India
articles.description:
Tesla will unveil its second product for India, which is the Tesla Model Y with a longer wheelbase. Called the Model Y L, this is a three-row, six-seater version of the five-seater Model Y. The Model Y L is also longer and has three rows, with an extra row of seats at the back. This model was first unveiled in China, while other markets have also received it recently.

Longer, More Premium and Feature-Rich

The Model Y L is much longer, with an increase of 186mm over the standard model. That also results in a longer wheelbase with more space. The range on offer for the Model Y L would be 680km, while the interior is tuned for more luxury. There is a dual-motor option only, which means the Model Y L will come with more power for the first time in India.


The interior would be more luxurious too, with a 19-speaker audio system, powered seats at the front with thigh extension, power adjustment for the second row, adaptive suspension and more. This would be a more premium product offering above the current Model Y and would be the flagship Tesla in India. Hence, prices would be more premium over the five-seater version.


Expect more details to be revealed when the car is unveiled in India on the 22nd of this month. With a more luxurious cabin and more space, the Model Y L could be an ideal option for luxury SUV buyers in India.

articles.title: How fears of AI disruption are reshaping career choices and increasing interest in graduate education among youth
articles.description: Amid rising concerns over AI disrupting entry-level jobs, many young graduates are considering graduate school as a strategic career move. A CNBC report highlights that this trend is emerging despite a stable economy, driven by fears about future employability. Experts say students now view higher education as a hedge against uncertainty, though concerns about cost and return on investment persist, making graduate school a more deliberate and career-focused decision than before.

articles.title: Tesla CEO Elon Musk: AI will mean everyone can have a penthouse if they ...
articles.description: Elon Musk reignited debate on universal basic income, proposing it as a solution to AI-driven job losses. He envisions a future of extreme abundance where AI and robotics will generate vast goods and services, making even luxuries like penthouses widely accessible without inflation. Musk previously predicted no poverty, only universal high income.

articles.title: Why mastering AI prompt engineering is becoming essential for students navigating personal finance decisions in a digital world
articles.description: As more young professionals turn to AI for financial advice, experts stress the importance of writing effective prompts to get reliable results. According to CNBC, MIT professor Andrew Lo highlights that while AI can explain broad financial concepts, it struggles with precise personal calculations and may produce misleading answers. Developing prompt engineering skills, verifying outputs, and applying critical thinking are essential for students and early-career professionals using AI as a financial planning tool.

articles.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang warns: It would be a 'horrible outcome' for America if China ...
articles.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang warns that the US risks losing AI dominance if it restricts chip sales to China, potentially ceding the market to rivals like Huawei. He argues that open-source models optimized for non-American hardware pose a significant threat, urging balanced regulations to allow US companies to compete globally and maintain technological leadership.
